events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need decide compliance for ABS-to-PVC glued with wrong cement, rough-complete, IPC, mfr listings. Need think as inspector.

Need produce valid JSON with fields. Need include decision fail if dangerous/non-compliant. Risk maybe high? ABS-to-PVC transition glued with wrong cement. Need know plumbing code. IPC Chapter 3 plastic pipe? IPC 305? Actually IPC Section 312? Let's recall.

ABS and PVC are different plastics. Transition cement? Traditionally, ABS to PVC transition cement exists (one-step transition?) but many codes prohibit? Need be precise. IPC (International Plumbing Code) plastic pipe joints: ABS pipe and fittings? Section 312? Let's recall IPC 2021: Chapter 12? Actually plumbing code piping: Section 312? Let's search memory.

IPC Chapter 3 General Regulations: Section 312 Protection of pipes? Section 314? Maybe P300? DWV plastic: Section 702? It references ASTM D2661 for ABS, D2665 PVC, D3033? Joints: Section 312.5? Let's recall from IPC 2018:
- 312.1 Excavation, 312.2 Preparation, 312.3 Joints, 312.4 Plastic pipe, etc?
No.

Maybe UPC has Table 14-1? IPC: Plastic pipe and fittings shall be joined by methods indicated in Table 312.8? Hmm.

Need assess "ABS-to-PVC glued with wrong cement." There is issue: ABS-to-PVC transition cement only allowed per manufacturer? Common "transition cement" may be listed for joining ABS to PVC? The "wrong cement" likely means regular ABS cement or regular PVC cement used to join ABS to PVC, not approved transition cement. This is non-compliant and installed-defective joint.

IPC prohibits joining different plastics except with approved transition cement? Let's recall exact standards. IPC 2015/2018 Section 312.4 Plastic pipe. "Joints in plastic piping shall be made in accordance with the manufacturer's instructions." Or Table 312.1? Let me recall exact text:

IPC Section 312 "Protection of Pipes". 312.1 Excavation. 312.2 Preparation of trench. 312.3 Joints. "The installation of a piping system shall be in accordance with the manufacturer's installation instructions." 312.4 Plastic pipe. "Plastic pipe shall be joined by methods listed in Table 312.4." Table? "Mechanical joints", "Solvent cementing", "Heat fusion", "Threaded joints". It might say "Solvent cementing shall be with listed cement."

IPC Section 702.1 Sanitary drain pipe. Table 702.1 lists ABS DWV ASTM D2661; PVC DWV ASTM D2665, D2949, etc. Section 705 "Joints and connections" maybe: 705.2 ABS plastic pipe and fittings joints: "Joints between ABS plastic pipe and fittings shall comply with Sections 705.2.1 through 705.2.3." 705.2.1 Solvent cement. "Solvent cement joints for ABS pipe and fittings shall comply with ASTM D2235." Actually ASTM D2235 is ABS cement. "Joint surfaces shall be clean and free from moisture. Solvent cement shall comply with ASTM D2235." 705.3 PVC: solvent cement ASTM D2564? Transition? Section 705.16? "Joints between different types of plastic pipe" maybe: "Mechanical joints shall be installed in accordance with manufacturer's instructions." There is "Joints between ABS and PVC" not allowed by solvent cement except listed transition cement? I remember IPC 705.1.2? Let's recall:

For drainage, vents, water distribution. IPC 2018 Section 705 "Joints and Connections." 
- 705.1 General. "Joints and connections shall be approved and of the type utilized for the specific piping material involved." 
- 705.1.1 Mechanical joints. 
- 705.1.2 Plastic pipe joints? "Plastic pipe and fittings shall be joined by methods indicated in Table 312.8."?

I should reason from manufacturer listings. ABS to PVC cement: Oatey sells "ABS-to-PVC transition cement" maybe "Green"> It is listed for ABS-to-PVC transition DWV? But many say not code approved in some jurisdictions; IPC allows transition cement only if listed for both pipe materials? If wrong cement, fail.

Need output fields: decision fail; risk maybe high or critical? Let's evaluate risk: installed, operating asset? rough-complete but not clos…
